core_model.structure_summary: A scalar self-consistent discrete-time map in which the next value of an aggregate quantity is a convex combination of two shifted sigmoidal (logistic-CDF) responses evaluated at the current aggregate value shifted by an offset, with the two branches carrying opposite-sign feedback.

variables.meaning: aggregate fraction/mean of the binary unit states
mixing fraction weighting the negative-feedback branch
steepness of the sigmoidal response (inverse temperature)

notes: Core is a mean-field self-consistent 1D iterated map; each unit responds to the population average, and the sign of the feedback term differs between the two population fractions (positive- vs negative-feedback branches). Fixed points and their stability give the equilibria and cycles referenced in the abstract. The sigmoidal response is a logistic CDF (P(u)=beta/(2 cosh^2) integrated).
